back_translation: The LORD said to Moses, 'Say to Aaron: Stretch out your hand with your staff over the rivers, canals, and ponds, and make the frogs come up onto the land of Egypt.'
Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt; the frogs came up and covered the land of Egypt.
The magicians did the same by their secret arts; they also brought up the frogs onto the land of Egypt.
Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron and said, 'Pray to the LORD that he take away the frogs from me and from my people; then I will let the people go that they may sacrifice to the LORD.'
Moses said to Pharaoh, 'You may have the honor over me. When shall I pray for you, your servants, and your people, that the frogs be cut off from you and your houses, and remain only in the Nile?'
He said, 'Tomorrow.' Moses said, 'As you say, so that you may know that there is none like the LORD our God.'
'The frogs shall depart from you, your houses, your servants, and your people; they shall remain only in the Nile.'
Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh, and Moses cried out to the LORD concerning the frogs which he had set against Pharaoh.
The LORD did according to the word of Moses; the frogs died out of the houses, courtyards, and fields.
They piled them in heaps upon heaps, and the land stank.
When Pharaoh saw that there was relief, he hardened his heart and did not listen to them, just as the LORD had said.
The LORD said to Moses, 'Say to Aaron: Stretch out your staff and strike the dust of the earth, that it may become gnats throughout the land of Egypt.'
They did so; Aaron stretched out his hand with his staff and struck the dust of the earth; gnats came upon man and beast. All the dust of the earth became gnats throughout the land of Egypt.
The magicians tried to bring forth gnats by their secret arts, but they could not. So the gnats remained on man and beast.
The magicians said to Pharaoh, 'This is the finger of God.' But Pharaoh's heart was hardened, and he did not listen to them, just as the LORD had said.
The LORD said to Moses, 'Rise early in the morning and stand before Pharaoh; behold, he goes out to the water, and say to him: Thus says the LORD, Let my people go that they may serve me.'

'For if you do not let my people go, behold, I will send swarms of flies upon you, your servants, your people, and your houses; the houses of the Egyptians shall be full of the swarms of flies, and the very ground on which they stand.'
'I will set apart on that day the land of Goshen where my people dwell, so that no swarms of flies shall be there, that you may know that I am the LORD in the midst of the land.'
'I will put a distinction between my people and your people. By tomorrow this sign will occur.'
The LORD did so; a heavy swarm of flies came into Pharaoh's house, the houses of his servants, and throughout the land of Egypt the land was ruined because of the swarms of flies.
Pharaoh called to Moses and Aaron and said, 'Go, sacrifice to your God within the land.'

Moses said, 'It is not right to do so, for what we sacrifice to the LORD our God would be an abomination to the Egyptians. Behold, if we sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians before their eyes, would they not stone us?'
'A three-day journey we shall go into the wilderness and sacrifice to the LORD our God as he tells us.'
Pharaoh said, 'I will let you go to sacrifice to the LORD your God in the wilderness, only do not go very far. Pray for me.'

Moses said, 'Behold, I am going out from you, and I will pray to the LORD, and the swarms of flies will depart from Pharaoh, his servants, and his people tomorrow; only let Pharaoh not deal deceitfully again by not letting the people go to sacrifice to the LORD.'
Moses went out from Pharaoh and prayed to the LORD.
The LORD did according to the word of Moses; he removed the swarms of flies from Pharaoh, his servants, and his people; not one remained.
But Pharaoh hardened his heart this time also and did not let the people go.
